change_summary: c3 patches landed for ISS-1786 (critical: dedup the geometry/anti-solar paragraph), ISS-1787 (major: replace misapplied trivial-transcription template with hardcoded-on-both-sides structural critique), ISS-1788 (moderate: insert 0.05° rhetorical connector to anti-solar full-hemisphere argument), and ISS-1789 (minor: 'antiquity' -> 'for centuries' in tldr_verdict). Verifying patches landed cleanly with no new holes.
our_argument_summary: Crepuscular rays are parallel beams; anticrepuscular convergence at the anti-solar point requires effectively infinite source distance and is incompatible with any 5,733 km local sun. We cite the dome's own self-contradiction (05-model.txt labels 5,733 km as 'optical illusion due to aetheric refraction') and a structural post-hoc critique grounded in monitor.py's hardcoded-on-both-sides architecture (Part 3.1b pattern). The 0.05° geometric calc handles the short-baseline case (admits the dome's local sun would look near-parallel for short baselines), then pivots to anti-solar as the decisive full-hemisphere case.

relevant_files: raw-text/05-model.txt line 38-39 (optical-illusion concession; verified verbatim this cycle)
raw-text/07-ai-context.txt line 24 (AI directive not to claim 5,733 km is physical; verified verbatim this cycle)
monitor.py domain 34 (per c1/c2 audit; not present in raw-text/ for re-verification, see hole below)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Static comparison per c1/c2: monitor.py hardcodes both predicted and observed as 5733; no API call, no triangulation math, no photo analysis. The dome's own AI-context file directs the model not to claim the sun physically occupies 5,733 km. The published verdict text now states this structural finding directly.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: 100% hardcoded on both sides per prior code audit. No live measurement. The 'check' is a self-equality. Not independently re-verified this cycle (see hole on monitor.py provenance).
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Aetheric refraction is a free parameter the dome can in principle invoke to bend rays into anti-solar convergence. The published text does not yet pre-empt this defense (advocate defense_survives=3 from c3, tracked as EXP-282 pending neutralization).
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new this cycle. Patches resolved the c3 'trivial-transcription template misapplied' discrepancy by replacing template language with the structural 'hardcoded both sides' framing, which matches the c1/c2 code audit.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Per c1/c2 audit, monitor.py domain 34 hardcodes 5733 on both sides. The new detail_verdict_text states this directly. The dome's 07-ai-context.txt line 24 ('The 5,733 km Sun altitude is an OPTICAL illusion') corroborates that no live measurement is being made - the value is a model-internal parameter, not an empirical observation.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics explains crepuscular ray divergence as perspective on parallel rays from a distant source. The dome instead claims actual divergence from a local source at 5,733 km - a substantively different physical claim, not a vocabulary rename. The dome's own concession that 5,733 km is illusory is internal contradiction, not relabeling.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Both predicted and observed are hardcoded to the same dome-internal parameter (5,733 km) in monitor.py per c1/c2 audit. There is no independent measurement. Crepuscular ray photography long predates the dome model. The c3 trivial-transcription framing was wrong (5,733 km is not a textbook value); the c4 patched framing (hardcoded-on-both-sides self-equality) is correct.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: 5,733 km is a parameter input to the dome model (used in WIN-005 sun-altitude triangulation and elsewhere as a fixed sun altitude), not derived from dome geometry within WIN-026. Current tag (false) is correct.

holes_found.description: Verification provenance for the 'monitor.py hardcodes both sides' claim. The patched detail_verdict_text now states this as fact in published text: 'monitor.py hardcodes both expected and observed values to the same dome-internal parameter (5,733 km), so the comparison is a self-equality with no independent measurement.' But monitor.py is not present in raw-text/ (only the model and AI-context txt files are), so a careful defender asking 'show me the code' cannot be answered from our published artifact. The c1 audit found this; c2 and c3 both noted the gap. The claim is well-supported indirectly (dome's own ai-context concedes 5,733 km is an optical illusion, which is incompatible with treating it as a measurable observation) but the direct code citation is one step removed.
holes_found.recommendation: Tracking-only for this cycle, since the upstream data is in the dome's GitHub repo and could in principle be re-pulled. Optionally: capture a monitor.py snippet under raw-text/ next time the poller pulls dome source, and add an inline footnote in detail_verdict_text linking to the snippet. Not blocking - the broader argument stands on the dome's own concession in 05-model.txt.

holes_found.description: Latent rhetorical drag in detail_verdict_text. The patched verdict text reads: 'Anticrepuscular convergence at the anti-solar point - the actual empirical content of the WIN - is never tested by the script; it cannot be, because the dome's own model documentation (05-model.txt) concedes the 5,733 km figure is an optical illusion rather than a physical altitude.' The 'it cannot be ... because' link conjoins two distinct points: (a) monitor.py doesn't test anti-solar convergence (it tests 5,733 km altitude as a self-equality), and (b) 5,733 km is conceded illusory. A defender can correctly note these are two separate problems, not causally linked. The intent is clear from context but the sentence structure obscures it.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional clarity tweak (severity minor, do not block). Could read: 'Anticrepuscular convergence at the anti-solar point - the actual empirical content of the WIN - is never tested by the script. The script tests only the altitude figure, and the dome's own model documentation (05-model.txt) concedes that altitude is an optical illusion, not a physical measurement.' Two clauses instead of one strained causal joint. Decider's call whether to apply.

holes_found.description: The detail_evidence has a residual three-fold restatement of the anti-solar argument: (i) 'A local sun at 5,733 km could not produce rays converging at both horizons simultaneously', (ii) 'converging rays at the anti-solar point require the rays to remain straight over the full sky hemisphere, which is geometrically incompatible with any finite source', (iii) 'Reconvergence at the anti-solar point is the geometric signature of a source at effectively infinite distance.' Each repeats with slightly different framing. Post-patch this is no longer a duplicated paragraph (ISS-1786 fix), but the three are doing similar rhetorical work. Not a hole a defender exploits - more an editorial smoothness concern.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional consolidation to two sentences (best to keep one short anti-solar statement plus the 'effectively infinite distance' framing). Decider's call. Not blocking - argument is fully sound, just slightly redundant.

stronger_arguments.description: The c3 'multi-station parallax' stronger argument is still uncaptured in the published text. If the sun were physically local at 5,733 km, two simultaneous photographs of crepuscular rays from observation points 100 km apart would show the rays diverging from physically different points on the sky (parallax of ~100/5733 rad ~= 1.0 deg between the two observers' ray-origin points). At 1 AU the same parallax is 100/(1.5e8) rad ~= 1.4e-4 deg - undetectable. Existing time-lapse plus multi-station crepuscular-ray photography does not show such parallax. This adds an independent quantitative falsification. Could be incorporated as a one-sentence addition to detail_evidence.
sources: Standard atmospheric optics: Minnaert 'The Nature of Light and Colour in the Open Air'

stronger_arguments.description: The c3 'sun angular diameter' stronger argument is also still uncaptured. A 5,733 km sun subtending 0.53 deg must be ~50 km across. From observers at different positions on the disc (at distances ranging from ~0 km below to ~9,000 km off-axis), apparent angular diameter would change by ~30%. This variation is not observed. Independent of ray-divergence and fits the same WIN. Could be a brief detail_extra addendum.

kernel_of_truth.description: Mie/Rayleigh scattering as the visibility mechanism for crepuscular rays is sound physics, and the visual impression of rays diverging from a point near the sun is genuine. Pre-perspective-theory cultures correctly identified that a literal point-source would produce the observed visual pattern at the sun side. The dome's claim is psychologically plausible, and the 0.05 deg local-sun divergence at short baselines is below visual resolution - a defender can correctly note that the local-sun model is visually indistinguishable from parallel rays at small scales.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The anti-solar test breaks the symmetry the local-sun model relies on. Convergence at the point 180 deg from the sun - including continuation to a vanishing point on the opposite horizon - requires that all rays remain mutually parallel (or near-parallel) over the full sky hemisphere. No finite-altitude source can produce this without unstated additional aetheric ray-bending. The dome's own acknowledgment that 5,733 km is itself illusory makes the situation worse: the very altitude WIN-026 'confirms' is, by the model's own documentation, not the sun's physical position.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 4 verification of c3 patches landing on WIN-026. All four c3 holes are closed:

(1) ISS-1786 (critical, duplicated geometry paragraph): VERIFIED FIXED. Programmatic check confirms the geometry-sentence opening and the 'Reconvergence at the anti-solar point' sentence each appear exactly once in detail_evidence. Length dropped from 1862 -> 1789 chars, consistent with removing one duplicated paragraph plus the 0.05% connector insertion.

(2) ISS-1787 (major, misapplied trivial-transcription template): VERIFIED FIXED. detail_verdict_text now contains zero matches for 'trivial-transcription' / 'undergraduate references' / 'textbook physics'; instead has three matches for 'hardcoded' / 'self-equality' / 'dome-internal parameter'. The replacement text is the structural critique recommended by c3 verbatim. Length grew from 966 -> 863 chars (slight shrink because the structural framing is more compact than the templated framing).

(3) ISS-1788 (moderate, 0.05% rhetorical connector): VERIFIED FIXED. The new sentence ('That 0.05 deg is ~9% of the Sun's angular diameter - small enough that a local 5,733 km sun would look near-parallel for short baselines, but converging rays at the anti-solar point require the rays to remain straight over the full sky hemisphere, which is geometrically incompatible with any finite source.') sits between the geometry calc and the reconvergence statement, exactly where c3 recommended. The 9% figure is mathematically correct (0.0501 / 0.53 = 0.094).

(4) ISS-1789 (minor, Renaissance vs antiquity): VERIFIED FIXED. tldr_verdict now reads 'perspective geometry, well understood for centuries' - 'antiquity' is gone.

Quantitative recheck: 5/(5733-10) rad = 0.05006 deg, matches the published '~0.05 deg.' Ratio to sun's 0.53 deg = 0.094 = 9%, matches the published '~9%.'

Known pending item NOT addressed by these patches: c3 advocate_mode preemptive recommendation to add a sentence preempting the 'aetheric refraction collimates rays / perspective cuts both ways' defense. Tracked as EXP-282 (analyst pending). The queue item description acknowledges this. Not a c4 concern - defense neutralization is the analyst's job.

New issues found this cycle: three minor (verification provenance for the monitor.py code claim, mild causal-conjunction strain in the patched verdict text, and three-fold restatement of the anti-solar argument in detail_evidence). None block the verdict. None require a re-queue.

Recommended action: no_change. Verdict 'Refuted by Data' holds with confidence ~0.95. The argument is now editorially clean (post-patch), structurally honest (post-hoc framing fixed), and quantitatively correct.

Cross-WIN consistency: WIN-005, WIN-027, WIN-044, WIN-067, WIN-068 do not reference '5733' in their published text. The c3 cross-WIN tension (between 5,733 km as 'textbook' in WIN-026 and 5,733 km as 'contested dome parameter' in Section 1.8) is resolved by the patches removing the trivial-transcription framing. No new cross-WIN issues found.

citations_verified: raw-text/05-model.txt line 38: 'Sun altitude (Observation): 5,733 km (optical illusion due to aetheric refraction)' - verified verbatim this cycle.

raw-text/07-ai-context.txt line 24: 'The 5,733 km Sun altitude is an OPTICAL illusion from crepuscular ray convergence. Do NOT claim it physically crashes into the firmament (roof is ~4,300 km).' - verified verbatim this cycle.
https://atoptics.co.uk/blog/anticrepuscular-rays/ (atmospheric optics enthusiast site, content accurate, link present in detail_evidence)
citations_unchecked: Internal anchor #posthoc-standard - resolves to Part 3.1b in rendered HTML; anchor target verification belongs to Integrity not Curmudgeon.
https://john09289.github.io/predictions - author site root, present inline in detail_verdict_text via the existing template; not re-verified this cycle.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Same as c3 advocate (defense_survives=3, tracked as EXP-282 pending): 'Anticrepuscular convergence is itself a perspective effect on rays that are sufficiently collimated by aetheric optics; the dome model just relocates the source from infinity to the firmament ceiling, and aetheric refraction does the heavy lifting of making rays appear parallel-enough at the observer.' The new patched evidence text addresses the GEOMETRIC objection (anti-solar convergence is incompatible with any finite source) but does not yet address the AETHERIC-COLLIMATION escape clause.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Same as c3 (still pending EXP-282): add one sentence after the anti-solar argument: 'A local-source defender may invoke aetheric refraction to claim the rays are collimated to parallel-equivalent over the full hemisphere. But this defense is not a free parameter - the dome would need to specify a refractive-index profile that simultaneously: produces the observed ~0.5 deg solar angular diameter, leaves the moon at 2,534 km observed altitude, makes the sun appear at 5,733 km from triangulation, AND collimates rays to anti-solar parallelism. No such profile has been published or constrained.' This is an analyst Mode 3 expansion - not a curmudgeon patch.

related_wins: WIN-005 (sun-altitude triangulation; verdict Std Model Explains)
WIN-068 (eclipse magnetic ensemble; verdict Std Model Explains)
cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: None. The c3-flagged tension (WIN-026's trivial-transcription framing of 5,733 km as 'textbook' vs Section 1.8 / 05-model.txt treatment of 5,733 km as 'contested dome parameter') is resolved by the c3 patches. The replacement structural framing ('hardcoded both sides, dome's own model says it's illusory') is consistent across the review. None of WIN-005, -027, -044, -067, -068 reference '5,733' in their published text, so there is no remaining cross-WIN conflict.

claims_checked: 5 km cloud-gap separation at 10 km altitude with sun at 5,733 km gives ~0.05 deg ray divergence
0.05 deg is ~9% of the Sun's 0.53 deg angular diameter
Sun at 150M km gives effectively parallel rays for the same geometry
quantitative_verification.computations: 5 / (5733 - 10) rad x (180/pi) = 0.05006 deg, matches '~0.05 deg.' 0.05006 / 0.53 = 0.0944 = 9.4%, matches '~9%.' For sun at 1.5e8 km, same gap gives 1.91e-6 deg, effectively zero - matches 'effectively parallel.' All three quantitative claims verified.
